title: anagallis caerulea var. verticillata
intro: Anagallis caerulea var. verticillata, also known as Blue Pimpernel or Blue Catchfly, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Anagallis caerulea var. verticillata include reducing inflammation and promoting the healing process of wounds, as well as providing antioxidant and antibacterial effects. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
